evidence: growing data suggest that these molecules are also involved in modulating the development and growth of GBM by acting on neuronal and glioblastoma stem cells.

literature.title: Coupling between GPR143 and dopamine D2 receptor is required for selective potentiation of dopamine D2 receptor function by L-3,4-dihydroxyphenylalanine in the dorsal striatum.

evidence: L-DOPA potentiates DA D2 receptor (DRD2) signaling through GPR143, the gene product of X-linked ocular albinism 1, a G-protein-coupled receptor for L-DOPA.

literature.title: Shaoyao Decoction reduced T lymphocyte activation by regulating of intestinal flora and 5-hydroxytryptamine metabolism in ulcerative colitis.

evidence: Reduced levels of 5-HT hindered the activation of T lymphocytes (H9 cells) via the PKC and NF-κB signaling pathways, by means of HTR1A (5-HT receptor 1A) and HTR3 (5-HT receptor 3).

literature.title: Tuning the Response of GPCR-Based Yeast Sensors Using Fluorescent Reporters.

evidence: Using the serotonin receptor 4 (HTR4)-based sensor as a testbed, we identify YPet, a yellow fluorescent protein previously evolved for improved intracellular fluorescence, as the optimal fluorescent reporter.

literature.title: Anti-angiogenic mechanisms and serotonergic dysfunction in the Rgs2 knockout model for the study of psycho-obstetric risk.

evidence: Whole-cell recordings from dorsal raphe serotonin neurons revealed altered 5-HT1A receptor-dependent inhibitory postsynaptic currents (5-HT1A-IPSCs) in female but not male KO neurons.

literature.title: Improved Split TEV GPCR β-arrestin-2 Recruitment Assays via Systematic Analysis of Signal Peptide and β-arrestin Binding Motif Variants.

evidence: split TEV GPCR β-arrestin-2 recruitment assays were optimized to monitor the endogenous ligand-induced activities of six GPCRs (DRD1, DRD2, HTR2A, GCGR, AVPR2, and GLP1R).

literature.title: Glucosylsphingosine Activates Serotonin Receptor 2a and 2b: Implication of a Novel Itch Signaling Pathway.

evidence: GS can activate mouse serotonin receptor 2a (mHtr2a) and 2b (mHtr2b), but not 2c (mHtr2c) that are expressed in HEK293T cells.

literature.title: Different roles of FAT10, FOXO1, and ADRA2A in hepatocellular carcinoma tumorigenesis in patients with alcoholic steatohepatitis (ASH) vs non-alcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH).

evidence: By using immunofluorescence study and quantitating the fluorescence intensity morphometrically in liver biopsied specimens from NASH and ASH patients, the protein expression of candidate molecules within hepatocytes cytoplasm are studied,

literature.title: Quantification of the mRNA expression of G protein-coupled receptors in human adipose tissue.

evidence: GPCRs are important regulators of adipose tissue endocrine and energy storage functions

literature.title: Dysregulated autoantibodies targeting vaso- and immunoregulatory receptors in Post COVID Syndrome correlate with symptom severity.

evidence: Severity of fatigue and vasomotor symptoms were associated with ADRB2 AAB levels in PCS/ME/CFS patients.

function: regulating the balance between bone formation and bone resorption

literature.title: Identification of gene biomarkers in patients with postmenopausal osteoporosis.

evidence: three genes (OSTF1, ADRB1 and NEO1) were speculated to serve roles in PMOP by regulating the balance between bone formation and bone resorption

literature.title: Dynamic mass redistribution reveals diverging importance of PDZ-ligands for G protein-coupled receptor pharmacodynamics.

evidence: a subset of GPCRs displayed diminished agonist efficacy in the absence of a PDZ ligand (i.e. HT2RB, ADRB1)

function: mediates sympathetic stimulation of heart rate and contractility

literature.title: Effect of Clinical and Genetic Factors on the Development of Postoperative Atrial Fibrillation After Coronary Artery Bypass Grafting (CABG) in Egyptian Patients Receiving Beta-Blockers.

evidence: Polymorphisms in the beta-1 adrenergic receptor (ADRB1) and G protein-coupled receptor kinase 5 (GRK5) genes are associated with variable responses to beta-blockers.

function: regulation of heart rate and myocardial contraction force

literature.title: Identification of phosphorylation sites on β1-adrenergic receptor in the mouse heart.

evidence: β1-adrenergic receptor (Adrb1) belongs to the superfamily of G-protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs) and plays a critical role in the regulation of heart rate and myocardial contraction force.

literature.title: Novel SNP Combination for Predictive Osteoporotic Diagnosis.

evidence: The functional impairment in osteodifferentiation was further validated in patient-derived cell lines harboring this triple-SNP combination.

literature.title: Uridine, a Therapeutic Nucleoside, Exacerbates Alcoholic Liver Disease via SRC Kinase Activation: A Network Toxicology and Molecular Dynamics Perspective.

evidence: According to network toxicology analysis, uridine interacts with important targets such as SRC, FYN, LYN, ADRB2, and GSK3B.

function: mediates proliferation and lethality in response to cholinergic agonist and antagonist

literature.title: Evidence of a M<sub>1</sub>-muscarinic GPCR homolog in unicellular eukaryotes: featuring Acanthamoeba spp bioinformatics 3D-modelling and experimentations.

evidence: Growth assays showed proliferation and lethal effects of exposure to mAChR1 agonist and antagonist respectively.

function: mediating extracellular signals through G proteins

literature.title: Integrating qHTS and QSAR Models to Identify Safe GPCR-Targeted Compounds: A Focus on hERG-Dependent Cardiotoxicity.
literature.journal: Journal of chemical information and modeling

evidence: Notable GPCRs such as ADRB2, CHRM1, DRD2, and HTR2A are important therapeutic targets linked to conditions ranging from asthma to schizophrenia.

literature.title: Investigating the Impact of Bisphenol A on Gastric Cancer Through Network Toxicology and Molecular Docking.

evidence: The protein-protein interaction network analysis revealed 19 hub genes, with core targets including MMP9, ADRB2, PTGS1, SLC6A4, ERBB2, MAPT, AR, SLC6A3, CNR1, PDE5A, DRD2, KCNH2, MC4R, CALCR, CHRM1, ADRB3, CXCR2, MMP1, and SHBG.

literature.title: Phenotypic profiling of mGlu<sub>7</sub> knockout mice reveals new implications for neurodevelopmental disorders.

evidence: One such gene, GRM7, encodes the metabotropic glutamate receptor 7 (mGlu<sub>7</sub> ), a G protein-coupled receptor that regulates presynaptic neurotransmitter release.

literature.title: Integrated Analysis of ATAC-Seq and RNA-Seq Reveals the Signal Transduction Regulation of the Molting Cycle in the Muscle of Chinese Mitten Crab (Eriocheir sinensis).

evidence: ADRB2 was unique to the pre-molt_vs_molt stage.

literature.title: Persistent challenges in the development of an mGlu<sub>7</sub> PAM in vivo tool compound: The discovery of VU6046980.

evidence: VU6046980 emerged as a putative in vivo tool compound with excellent CNS penetration (K<sub>p</sub> = 4.1; K<sub>p,uu</sub> = 0.7) and efficacy in preclinical models.

literature.title: Different responses to risperidone treatment in Schizophrenia: a multicenter genome-wide association and whole exome sequencing joint study.

evidence: The GWAS revealed a significant association between several SNPs and treatment response, such as three GRM7 SNPs (rs141134664, rs57521140, and rs73809055).

function: suppresses the cyclic AMP cascade after activation by glutamate

literature.title: A single nucleotide polymorphism in the metabotropic glutamate receptor 7 gene is associated with multiple sclerosis in Iranian population.

evidence: The metabotropic glutamate receptor 7 (GRM7) gene is a G protein-coupled receptor that suppresses the cyclic AMP cascade after activation by glutamate.

literature.title: Simultaneous activation of CXC chemokine receptor 4 and histamine receptor H1 enhances calcium signaling and cancer cell migration.

evidence: Simultaneous activation of CXCR4 and HRH1 synergistically increases calcium flux in MDA-MB-231 cells that endogenously express CXCR4 and HRH1 but not in cells deficient in CXCR4 or HRH1.

function: PTx detection via disruption of Gαi binding and compensatory Gαq-mediated calcium signaling

literature.title: Sensitive detection of pertussis toxin in acellular pertussis vaccines using HRH1-mediated calcium signaling.

evidence: The presence of PTx disrupts histamine-induced Gαi binding to HRH1, resulting in a compensatory increase in Gαq binding and enhanced calcium signaling.

literature.title: MRGPRX4 is a bile acid receptor for human cholestatic itch.

evidence: MRGPRX4 is expressed in human dorsal root ganglion (hDRG) neurons and co-expresses with itch receptor HRH1.

downstream_pathway: G-protein-coupled receptor or Akt signalling pathways

literature.title: Integrative analysis of small non-coding RNAs predicts a piRNA/miRNA-CCND1/BRAF/HRH1/ATXN3 regulatory circuit that drives oncogenesis in glioblastoma.

evidence: We conjectured that upregulated HRH1 and ATXN3 were targeted by both piRNAs and miRNAs, and along with BRAF and CCND1 might induce cell proliferation in GBM through G-protein-coupled receptor or
